title: Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary—also called Mayuravana ("Peafowl Forest") is a protected area situated in the Adichunchanagiri Hills near the Chunchunagiri Temple in Na
overview.description: Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary—also called Mayuravana ("Peafowl Forest") is a protected area situated in the Adichunchanagiri Hills near the Chunchunagiri Temple in Nagamangala Taluk, Mandya district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It along with Choolannuar are the only sanctuary in India declared specifically for the protection of the Indian peafowl.

topPlaces.name: Adichunchanagiri Hills
topPlaces.description: Adichunchanagiri, also called Mahasamsthana Math, is a hill township in Nagamangala Taluk, Mandya District, Karnataka State, India, 110 km west of the state capital Bangalore, on a rocky hill at an altitude of about 3,300 ft. above sea level.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (88 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (80 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adichunchanagiri Hills, Belluru Cross, Agachahalli, Dadaga.
